title: Xerosiphon angustiflorus
taxonomy: Xerosiphon angustiflorus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Xerosiphon angustiflorus is within the genus Xerosiphon, which is a group of evergreen shrubs and trees. Xerosiphon angustiflorus is also known to be a member of the order Magnoliales, a group of flowering plants that are often characterized by their distinctive seed pods. The classification of Xerosiphon angustiflorus is still subject to ongoing research and updates in the field of botany.
uses: Unfortunately, I was unable to find reliable information on the medicinal uses of Xerosiphon angustiflorus.

constituents: Xerosiphon angustiflorus contains saponins and flavonoids as its active constituents, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties.
